name: Copenhagen Market
description: A classic round-headed cabbage with pale green, tightly packed leaves. Produces solid, sweet heads ideal for fresh use, sauerkraut, and braising. One of the most dependable spring and fall cabbages.
history: Developed in Denmark in the early 1900s and imported to the US where it became a commercial standard. Its reliable heading and compact size made it a favorite for market gardeners and home growers alike.
seasonal_quirks: Best planted in early spring or late summer for fall harvest. Light frost improves sweetness significantly. Heads may split if left too long after maturity in wet weather.
harvest_cues: Head should feel firm and solid when squeezed. Outer leaves begin to look papery. Typical size is 6–8 inches across.
variety_notes: One of the best-flavored cabbages for fresh coleslaw and sauerkraut. Less prone to splitting than some large-headed types.
preservation: Ferments excellently into sauerkraut. Stores in root cellar conditions for several months.
